title: hebea parviflora
intro: Hebea parviflora, also known as the dwarf or New Zealand tea tree, is a flowering plant that belongs to the hebe family. Hebea parviflora is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections, and it is also believed to have antioxidant and cardiovascular-protective properties. Hebea parviflora can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and it is also used in herbal preparations, such as tinctures and ointments, to leverage its medicinal benefits.
